articles.title: Arfin India Ltd Hits All-Time High of Rs 97.17 as Momentum Builds Across Timeframes - Markets Mojo

articles.full_text:
Record-Breaking Price Movement
On 15 Apr 2026, Arfin India Ltd’s stock price surged to Rs 97.17, surpassing its previous 52-week high of Rs 85.00 by 14.37%. The stock opened with a gap up of 3.32% and recorded an intraday high gain of 3.5%, demonstrating strong buying interest throughout the trading session. Despite a day change of 0.66%, which was slightly below the Sensex’s 1.44% gain, the stock’s three-day consecutive rise has yielded a cumulative return of 10.39%.
Volatility was notably high, with an intraday volatility of 115.26% calculated from the weighted average price, indicating active trading and dynamic price movements. The stock currently trades above all key moving averages, including the 5-day, 20-day, 50-day, 100-day, and 200-day averages, reinforcing the prevailing bullish momentum.
Long-Term Outperformance Against Benchmarks
Arfin India Ltd’s price appreciation over various time horizons has been remarkable. The stock has delivered a staggering 234.16% return over the past year, vastly outperforming the Sensex’s modest 1.59% gain during the same period. Year-to-date, the stock has risen by 28.89%, contrasting with the Sensex’s decline of 8.53%. Over three years, the stock’s return of 359.63% dwarfs the Sensex’s 28.99%, while the five-year and ten-year returns stand at an extraordinary 1251.93% and 3285.07%, respectively, compared to the Sensex’s 59.72% and 204.18%.
This consistent outperformance highlights Arfin India Ltd’s ability to generate substantial shareholder value over the long term, positioning it as a standout performer within the Non-Ferrous Metals sector and the broader market.
Financial Strength and Growth Drivers
The company’s recent quarterly results underscore its operational strength. Net sales reached a record Rs 187.96 crores, while operating profit to net sales ratio hit a high of 7.39%. Profit before tax excluding other income (PBT less OI) surged by 221.6% to Rs 7.59 crores compared to the previous four-quarter average. Operating profit to interest coverage ratio also improved to 2.70 times, the highest recorded, indicating enhanced earnings quality and debt servicing capability.
Net profit growth of 135.12% in the December 2025 quarter further reflects the company’s positive earnings momentum. Quarterly profit after tax (PAT) reached Rs 5.09 crores, with earnings per share (EPS) at Rs 0.30, both marking quarterly highs. These figures contribute to the company’s healthy long-term growth profile, with operating profit growing at an annual rate of 31.33% and a five-year sales compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 16.30%.
Valuation and Quality Assessment
Despite the impressive price appreciation, Arfin India Ltd’s valuation metrics indicate a premium positioning. The price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio stands at 170 times trailing twelve months (TTM), while the price-to-book value (P/BV) is 9.94 times. Enterprise value multiples are also elevated, with EV/EBITDA at 46.97 times and EV/EBIT at 53.48 times. The enterprise value to capital employed ratio is 6.23 times, signalling a relatively expensive valuation compared to historical levels.
The dividend yield remains modest at 0.12%, with the latest dividend declared at Rs 0.11 per share and an ex-dividend date of 17 Nov 2025. The company’s return on capital employed (ROCE) is 10.7%, which, while positive, suggests room for improvement in capital efficiency.
Quality assessments rate the company as average overall, with good growth but below-average capital structure and moderate leverage. Institutional holdings are low at 2.71%, and domestic mutual funds hold no stake, which may reflect cautious positioning by larger investors despite the company’s strong performance.
Technical Indicators and Market Sentiment
The technical outlook for Arfin India Ltd remains bullish. The overall trend shifted to bullish on 8 Apr 2026 at a price of Rs 86.48, supported by positive weekly and monthly MACD and Bollinger Bands indicators. Moving averages confirm the upward trend, while the Dow Theory also signals bullish momentum. Some indicators such as the monthly RSI and weekly KST show mixed signals, but the dominant trend remains positive.
Key support levels include the 52-week low of Rs 23.06, while resistance levels previously at Rs 85.00 have been surpassed. Delivery volumes have increased significantly, with a 1-month delivery change of 81.08% and a 1-day delivery change of 14.03% compared to the five-day average, indicating strong market participation.
Summary of Arfin India Ltd’s Market Journey
Arfin India Ltd’s journey to its all-time high price of Rs 97.17 is characterised by sustained growth, strong quarterly financial results, and a bullish technical setup. The stock’s performance has consistently outpaced major indices and sector benchmarks, driven by robust sales growth, improved profitability, and operational efficiency.
While valuation metrics suggest a premium, the company’s financial strength and market position within the Non-Ferrous Metals sector underpin its current market valuation. The recent upgrade in mojo grade from Hold to Buy on 8 Apr 2026, with a mojo score of 70.0, reflects improved market sentiment and recognition of the company’s performance.
As of 15 Apr 2026, Arfin India Ltd stands as a micro-cap stock with a market cap grade reflecting its size, yet it continues to demonstrate market-beating returns and operational progress. The stock’s ability to maintain gains above key moving averages and its record-setting quarterly results highlight a well-established upward trajectory.
Conclusion
Arfin India Ltd’s attainment of an all-time high price marks a significant milestone in its market history. The company’s strong financial results, consistent growth, and positive technical indicators have collectively contributed to this achievement. While valuation remains elevated, the stock’s performance over multiple time frames and its leadership within the Non-Ferrous Metals sector underscore its noteworthy market standing as of April 2026.
